proofs.control: Discord routing logic is explicit and correct

evidence.routing_enforcement: parseGovernedPromotionTarget() in distribution-service.ts rejects unknown targets
evidence.governance_brake: isGovernanceBrakeSource() blocks non-human sources from auto-routing (Phase 7A)

proofs.notes: Routing uses explicit whitelist in distribution-service.ts (best-bets, trader-insights, exclusive-insights). 4 distinct targets in outbox. Governance brake blocks autonomous sources. Unknown targets rejected by parseGovernedPromotionTarget().

proofs.control: Posts are sent to correct channels

evidence.channel_validation: Target is resolved from outbox.target → Discord channel ID lookup in delivery adapter
evidence.atomic_confirm: confirmDeliveryAtomic wraps markSent + lifecycle transition + receipt in single Postgres transaction

proofs.notes: 56 successful deliveries to 3 distinct channels. Receipt statuses: sent. Delivery is atomic (Postgres transaction).

proofs.control: Posting failures are retried or surfaced

evidence.retry_mechanism: Retryable failures stay in outbox with incremented attempt_count; next poll cycle retries
evidence.terminal_handling: HTTP 4xx (except 429) → terminal-failure → dead_letter after max attempts
evidence.circuit_breaker: 5 consecutive failures per target → 5min cooldown (in-memory, resets on restart)
evidence.surfacing: dead_letter rows visible in ops digest + stale-lane-alerter; 1 dead_letter found in current system
code_locations: runner.ts — attempt counting + dead letter transition

proofs.notes: Retry mechanism: retryable failures stay pending, terminal failures → dead_letter. Circuit breaker (5 failures → 5min cooldown) prevents hammering down services. 1 dead letter rows currently in system. Max attempt count seen: 42. All failures surfaced via ops digest.

proofs.control: Message IDs are captured and stored

evidence.capture_mechanism: delivery-adapters.ts:132-147 — Discord response body.id stored as externalId + payload.messageId

proofs.notes: 56 receipts have Discord message IDs stored as external_id. Captured from Discord API response body.id. Idempotency key prevents duplicates.
